content:
Will Rust need to support both of these options for linux?

The fedora x86-64 kernel is using fentry for dynamic ftrace and not using patchable entries for dynamic ftrace. Is that a fedora-ism? Is there a reason to use both on some architectures?
I don't know if the right flags are passed to rustc, but I'm pretty sure we added the flags required by patchable function entry a long time ago.
so it should just be a matter of adding flags, if not already there
There's no support for mcount in Rust I think? It looks like x86 does not use patchable function entry for it (many other archs do)

There is some support for mcount (not fentry), but no nop or recording of call sites. llvm only supports nop and record for s390x with fentry. I've opened an LLVM PR to enable it for x86 and fentry.

Looking through the kernel history, patchable functions entries can be used for purposes other than ftrace. One of which is bpf dispatcher functions on x86.
